title: Proof termination checker too restrictive — structural recursion only
description: The proof termination checker only accepts structural recursion: the recursive call must be on an obviously smaller sub-term. This rejects proofs that are mathematically sound but use a non-obvious termination argument (e.g. well-founded relations, lexicographic order, measure functions). A proof writer implementing a valid proof must either restructure it unnaturally to satisfy the checker, or give up and use trusted — which defeats the point of writing the proof. The termination checker must either support well-founded recursion with user-supplied measure functions, or the spec must document exactly which patterns are accepted so proof authors know what is achievable.
